text: This is gonna put r/cars in crisis mode

0.93 g of grip that's high crazy thing is a 2005 Ferrari f430 produces 0.95-0.98 g's in the skid pad. With the advancement of tires, suspension, and cars. You can get good sized Sedans with the grip levels of early 2000s sports cars.

That's just sad. Not saying Z cars aren't still good, but it sucks to see where they're at nowadays.

That’s great, but peak corner Gs are not the same thing as handling. A 90s Miata may not pull many Gs either, but handles great by most accounts.
Gotta give credit where credit is due. Good job Nissan engineers. That's pretty damn commendable in my book.
Breaking news: run flats are horrible.
